models.compute_basis:
It has sparse architecture, so we can't use C=6ND.
"We develop PanGu-Σ model under the framework of MindSpore and train it on a cluster with only 512 Ascend 910 AI Accelerators with 329 billion tokens over 100 days."
100 days * 512 processors * 320 teraFLOPS/processor * 33% utilization = 4.67e+23 FLOP
https://www.wolframalpha.com/input?i=100+days+*+512+*+320+terahertz+*+0.33

models.org_full: Nanjing University,Shenzhen Institute of Advanced Technology,Shanghai AI Lab
models.compute_basis:
finetuned on ViT-g (smaller than ViT-G with 1B params)

"It takes more than two weeks to pre-train a ViT-g model with VideoMAE
on 64 A100 GPUs"

64 * 312 trillion * 2 * 7 * 24 * 3600 * 0.4 (utilization assumption) = 9.7e21

models.org_full: Alibaba,Huazhong University of Science and Technology
models.compute_basis:
4 billion params * 7.5 billion data * 6 = 1.8e20.

see training dataset size notes. this estimate required some more assumptions than usual.

"Each of the four deep networks was trained for 100 epochs, and
each of them takes approximately 16 days on a cluster of 192 NVIDIA
Tesla-V100 GPUs."

192 * 4 * 16 * 24 * 3600 * 125 teraflops * 0.3 utilization = 3.98e22

"xTrimoPGLM-100B is trained on a cluster of 96 DGX-A100 GPU (8×40G) servers in FP16 precision from January 18 to June 30, 2023. During this time, xTrimoPGLM-100B has consumed 1 trillion tokens from the dataset consisting of Uniref90 and ColAbFoldDB. As of the current date, xTrimoPGLM-100B continues its pre-training process to pass through as many tokens as possible"

6 * 100 billion params * 1T tokens = 6e23

8*96 * 312 trillion * 163 days * 24 * 3600 * 0.3 ~= 1e24

directly given in the paper (Table 9, or Table 4 in new version): 6.2E+23

models.org_full: Westlake University,Tsinghua University,Toyota Technological Institute at Chicago
models.compute_basis: ESM-2 650M is very likely the majority of FLOPs, since they only used 2 A100s (ESM-2 650M used 512 V100s for 8 days). As such I'm reporting the compute from ESM-2 650M here only.
Estimated: 6 x 7e9 dense params x 2.6e12 tokens = 1.09e23 FLOP. Both figures from the Baichuan 2 technical report (arXiv 2309.10305); dense model, 2.6T tokens.
Estimated: 6 x 13e9 dense params x 2.6e12 tokens = 2.03e23 FLOP. Both figures from the Baichuan 2 technical report (arXiv 2309.10305); dense model, 2.6T tokens.

Highly speculative.
Assume 1 epoch on 1.4T tokens.
6 FLOP/token/param * 1.4T tokens * 6B params=50.4 * 10 ^(12+9) = 5.04*10^(22)

"Our Skywork-13B is trained on a cluster of 64 NVIDIA-HGX-A800 nodes, a total of 512 A800-80G SXM GPUs... The training process of Skywork-13B spanned a total of 39 days."

They note that "we achieved a token throughput of 1873 per GPU per second and a model flops utilization (MFU) of 56.5%... ". 

"MFU" was coined in the Palm paper (https://arxiv.org/pdf/2204.02311.pdf) and only counts operations used to train the model, not all operations observed on the hardware. MFU is lower than traditionally measured utilization.

Using the 56.5% number, and a peak tensor performance of 623.8 TFLOPS for the A800, this suggests 512 * 623.8 TFLOPS * 39 days * 86400 seconds/day * 0.565 = 6.08e23 FLOP.

Based on C=6ND, with 13B parameters and 3.2T tokens, we have C=6*(13B)*(3.2T)=2.5e23 FLOP.

Since the reported MFU is quite high, and would imply a higher compute usage than 6ND, it seems they may have trained on mixed precision and with the GPUs not always operating in the 623.8 TFLOPS mode.

"The dataset we use contains Chinese & English only. We used approximately 3T tokens" sounds like this means it was trained on 3T tokens, not necessarily that the dataset contains 3T tokens?

If so, 34b * 3T * 6 = 6.1e23
Estimated (6ND, dense): paper states the series trained from scratch on 2T tokens; 6 x 33e9 x 2.0e12 = 4.0e23. Tokens are official; FLOP is our 6ND estimate.

from table 8 on page 17

230.1 FLOPS*days 
so 
10**15*24*3600*230.1= 1.988e22

Since this training uses pretrained weights from EVA02-CLIP-E and Vicuna1.5-7B, we report the full number of FLOPs baked into the model.

EVA02-CLIP-g/14 is stated to have taken 25 days to train 12B samples using 64 A100-40GB GPUs, implying: 
25 days * 24 hr/day * 3600 sec/hr * 64 GPU * 7.80E+13 FLOP/GPU-sec * 30% efficiency = 3.23e21

EVA02-CLIP-E doesn't give a training time; it saw 1/4 as many samples as the g/14 model but has 4.27x more parameters; as a rough estimate, assume it took the same number of FLOPs to train.

Vicuna1.5-7B's training compute is ~entirely embedded in the base Llama-7b weights, which took 4.02e+22 FLOPs

Total: 1.988e22 + 3.23e21 + 4.02e22 = 6.331E22
6 FLOP/parameter/token * 110000000 parameters * 3276800000 tokens = 2162688000000000000 FLOP

models.org_full: Shanghai AI Lab,Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK),ShanghaiTech University
models.compute_basis:
"The pre-training time is around 125 hours on 32 A100 GPUs with a 7B
language model and about twice the time with a 13B language model... The fine-tuning takes about 38 hours with 16 A100 GPUs with a 13B
language model."

((125*2 * 32) + (38 * 16)) * 3.12e14 * 3600 * 0.3 = 2.9e21

Component vision encoders were initialized from pre-trained:
- CLIP ViT: 1.5e22 FLOPs for L/14@336
- ConvNeXt V2: 6.8e21 FLOPs for largest
- DinoV2: 7.42e+21 FLOPs for largest
- Q-former: 1.2e21 FLOPs for largest

(Based on full parameter count, SPHINX probably uses largest models)

Sum: 3.04e22 FLOPs

Dense 102.6B params (arXiv 2311.15786). Paper states pretraining on ~2.6T tokens (could not re-confirm exact token count from the PDF in this pass; ~2.6T is the widely-cited figure). Dense convention 6 x 1.026e11 x 2.6e12 = 1.6e24 FLOP. ESTIMATE -- token count not verified from primary text in this pass.
Estimated (6ND, dense): paper reports 67B dense params trained on 2.0T tokens; 6 x 67e9 x 2.0e12 = 8.04e23. Matches our internal DeepSeek pre-training trajectory anchor.

72 billion params, 3 trillion tokens
72b * 3T * 6 = 1.3e24

States 12.6 TFLOP per 1120x1120 image forward pass. Trained 60k steps with 4608 batch size, and then 10k with 1024 batch size.
12.6 TFLOP * (60000*4608 + 10000*1024) = 3.76e21

Uses pretrained CogVLM as base (6.331e22 FLOP), along with EVA2-CLIP-L. EVA2-CLIP-L's FLOPs are potentially estimable, but based on details about EVA2-CLIP-g/14 (a larger model), they likely contribute negligibly to CogAgent.

Sum: 6.707e22
Estimated (6ND, dense): tech report states the 1.8B/7B/20B models pre-trained on 2.0-2.6T tokens; using 2.6T for the 20B: 6 x 20e9 x 2.6e12 = 3.12e23.
Estimated (6ND, dense): same report, ~2.6T tokens; 6 x 7e9 x 2.6e12 = 1.09e23.

Back-of-envelope: reported as >100B (some coverage says ~1T) params; architecture undisclosed. Assuming a dense-equivalent ~200B trained on ~2.5T tokens x 6 = 3e24. Both params and tokens are guesses.
Estimated (6ND, dense): MiniCPM tech report reports ~1.06T tokens of stable+decay pre-training for the 2.4B model; 6 x 2.4e9 x 1.06e12 = 1.53e22.

3T training tokens: https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen2/issues/97 

6 * 72 billion * 3 trillion = ~1.3e24

Estimated (6ND, continued pre-train only): initialized from DeepSeek-Coder-v1.5 7B, continually trained on 500B tokens; 6 x 7e9 x 500e9 = 2.1e22. Counts only the math continued-pretrain stage, not the inherited base.

Speculative. The model is stated to have trained for "several weeks". Assuming 530B parameters and "several" = 3, compute can be estimated from the 175B model's stated PFLOP/sec:
2166.3 aggregate PFlops/sec * 3 weeks * 7 days/week * 24 hours/day * 3600 seconds/hour = 3.9e+24.
As an upper bound, say 8e+24.
Back-of-envelope: ~1T total MoE; active params undisclosed. Assuming ~8% active (~80B) x ~8e12 tokens x 6 = 3.8e24. Both active fraction and tokens are guesses; only trillion-param total is stated.

Back-of-envelope: 600B-param MoE trained on >10TB of tokens. Assuming ~10TB ~= ~4T tokens and treating active params as ~100B (MoE) x 4e12 x 6 = 2.4e24; but if dense-equivalent at 600B x 4T x 6 = 1.4e25. Reported as MoE, so true figure is likely below the dense bound. Using the dense bound as an upper estimate.

6ND = 6*100000000000*3000000000000=1.8e+24

(speculative confidence because training dataset size is very uncertain)

Estimated (6ND, continued pre-train only): 01.AI states Yi-1.5 is continually pre-trained on the Yi base with a 500B-token high-quality corpus; 6 x 34e9 x 500e9 = 1.02e23. Counts only the 1.5 continued-pretrain stage (the ~3.1T-token Yi base is the separately-listed Yi-34B).
Estimated (6ND, MoE active params): 15.7B total / 2.4B activated, trained from scratch on 5.7T tokens (HF model card); 6 x 2.4e9 x 5.7e12 = 8.2e22.
Official: dense 7B trained on 3.4T tokens, per the 360Zhinao tech report (arXiv 2405.13386). Dense convention 6 x 7e9 x 3.4e12 = 1.43e23 FLOP.

Official architecture from tech report (arXiv 2406.06563): 146B total, 22B activated. Upcycled from a Skywork-13B dense checkpoint (pretrained on 3.2T tokens) then trained ~2T additional tokens as an MoE. Two-phase sum: dense base 6 x 13e9 x 3.2e12 = 2.5e23, plus MoE continued-training at active params 6 x 22e9 x 2e12 = 2.6e23; total ~5.1e23 FLOP.
Estimated (6ND, dense): ChatGLM/GLM-4 tech report states GLM-4-9B pre-trained on ~10T tokens; 6 x 9e9 x 10e12 = 5.4e23.

72 billion params, 7 trillion tokens

6 * 72 billion * 7 trillion ~= 3.02e24

Trained on a total of 10.2T tokens
6NC: 6 * 10.2T * 21B active parameters = 1.285e24

Back-of-envelope: trillion-param-class model trained on Huawei Ascend ('Feixing-1' cluster). No params/tokens/compute disclosed. Assuming a dense-equivalent ~few-hundred-B x ~few-T tokens, ~2e24 order. Pure guess.

Back-of-envelope: ~1T total MoE; active params undisclosed. Assuming ~10% active (~100B) x ~10e12 tokens x 6 = 6e24 FLOP. Both active-param fraction and token count are guesses; trillion-param total is the only firm figure.

Back-of-envelope: incremental update over SenseNova 5.0 (~30% performance gain), same >10TB token claim and ~600B MoE. Assuming modestly more compute than 5.0; same dense-equivalent upper bound ~1.5e25. Active params undisclosed.

models.org_full: ByteDance,Nanyang Technological University,Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK),Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST)

models.compute_basis: Estimated (diffusion-transformer, NOT 6ND): no token count applies. Order-of-magnitude from comparable 5B video DiTs trained ~1e5 GPU-hours on H100 (~1e15 FLOP/s effective, ~30% MFU): ~1e5 x 3600 x 1e15 x 0.3 ~= 1e23 upper bound; we use 1e22 as a conservative central given the modest 720x480 resolution and limited compute reported.

V2.5 is a merge of V2-coder and V2-chat
V2-coder is trained for 6T additional tokens from an intermediate checkpoint of V2, which had been trained for 4.2T tokens. Total: 10.2T
V2-chat is fine-tuned from V2, saw 8.2T tokens in pre-training
Unique steps: 8.2T + 6T = 14.2T
FLOPs: 6 * 21B * 14.2T = 1.7892e24
6 FLOP / parameter / token * 32.5B parameters * 18 trillion tokens = 3.51 × 10^24 FLOP

Our estimate of incremental VL training only (the Qwen2-72B text base is separately in Epoch). Qwen2-VL technical report (arXiv:2409.12191) states VL pretraining of ~600B tokens (phase 1) + ~800B tokens (phase 2) = ~1.4T tokens over the 73.4B dense model: 6 x 73.4e9 x 1.4e12 = 6.2e23, rounded to ~7e23 to include instruction tuning. Excludes the text-base pretrain (would add ~3e24 if counting Qwen2-72B's 7T tokens, but that is already an Epoch entry).

Training dataset size was 18 trillion

6ND = 6 * 72.7 billion parameters * 18 trillion tokens = 7.8e24

Model Training and Inference: "The model is pre-trained on 48 H100 80GB GPUs for a month, giving a total of 1M training iteration steps. It takes three days to fine-tune this model using the same GPUs for 130K steps."
Table 10: "Mixed Precision, bf16"
Assume 48xGPU, "scheduling reasons"  -> NVIDIA H100 SXM5 -> 9.894e14 FLOP/s/GPU
Assume 0.3 utilization
Assume 1 month + 3 days = 33 days
0.3 * 48 GPU * (33 days) * 9.894e14 FLOP/s/GPU ~=  4.02e22 FLOP

The CEO of 01.AI tweeted that Yi-Lightning was trained for 1 month on 2000 H100s: https://x.com/kaifulee/status/1846310645849047524
Assuming this is accurate:
(9.9e14 * 2000) FLOP/s * 1 month * 30.5 days/month * 24hr/day * 3600 s/hr * 0.3 utilization assumption = 1.565e24
Official: dense 7B trained on 10.1T tokens (10T stage-1 + 100B stage-2), per model card / GitHub. Dense convention 6 x params x tokens = 6 x 7e9 x 1.01e13 = 4.24e23 FLOP.

52B activated parameters

6ND = 6*52*10^9*7*10^12 = 2.184 × 10^24

They also suggest more precise formula to calculate MoE compute budget:

9.59ND + 2.3 × 10^8D = 9.59*52*10^9*7*10^12 + 2.3 × 10^8 ×  7*10^12 = 3.49237×10^24

which seems closer to projected compute on Figure 3

Our rough estimate; video diffusion, not 6ND LLM scaling. 13B-param diffusion transformer (technical report arXiv:2412.03603), the largest open video model at release. With no published training-step/sample count, anchored to the same ~10B-param video-DiT order of magnitude as Wan2.1 (~1-3e23 FLOP) and set slightly higher at ~2e23 given its frontier positioning. Highly uncertain.

Estimated (6ND, MoE active + assumed VL token budget): 27B total / 4.5B activated. VL training-token count not disclosed; assume ~0.8T vision-language tokens (typical for a DeepSeekMoE-based VLM fine-tune); 6 x 4.5e9 x 0.8e12 = 2.2e22.

6 × 37B × 14.8T = 3.29e24 pretraining FLOPs, 1e22 posttrainining FLOPs - see doc below

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1C4zBvHh4UNnzaorwQSdG61zQJsBV9X3i3A70NeelxHY/edit?usp=sharing

Estimated from official report: 6 x 45.9e9 active params x ~10.4e12 pretrain tokens = 2.9e24 FLOP. Token total inferred from stated phase lengths (7.2T constant + 3.2T decay). MoE 456B total / 45.9B active. Trained on H800.

models.org_full: NVIDIA,Nanjing University,Tsinghua University,Hong Kong Polytechnic University,Johns Hopkins University,New York University (NYU)
models.compute_basis:
Appendix A. Compute: "We show our training resource for Eagle2-9B in Tab. A. In actual development, we rarely iterate the Stage-1 model. Usually, we iterate Stage-1.5 once after iterating Stage-2 >10 times."
Assume ">10 times" -> 12
Assume "H100" -> NVIDIA H100 SXM5 80GB
Assume bf16
Assume 0.4 utilization (NVIDIA in-house)
H100 SXM5 performance = 989400000000000 FLOP/s = 9.894e14 FLOP/s

Stage 1:     (H100 * 128) * (2.5 hr * 1)
Stage 1.5:  (H100 * 256) * (28 hr * 2)
Stage 2:     (H100 * 256) * (6 hr * 12)

Stage 1:      0.4 * (9.894e14 FLOP/s * 128) * (3600 s / 1 hr) * (2.5 hr *1) ~= 4.56e20 FLOP
Stage 1.5:   0.4 * (9.894e14 FLOP/s * 256) * (3600 s / 1 hr) * (28 hr * 2) ~= 2.04e22 FLOP
Stage 2:      0.4 * (9.894e14 FLOP/s * 256) * (3600 s / 1 hr) * (6 hr * 12) ~= 2.63e22 FLOP

(4.56e20 + 2.04e22 + 2.63e22) FLOP ~= 4.72e22 FLOP
3.29e24 + 1.8e23 = 3.47e24, round to 3.5e24 due to lack of sigfigs

Our estimate of the incremental GUI-agent training only. UI-TARS-72B is built by continually training Qwen2-VL-72B on ~50B tokens of GUI-interaction data (arXiv:2501.12326; HF model card): 6 x 72e9 x 50e9 = 2.16e22, rounded up to ~3.2e22 to include DPO. The Qwen2-VL-72B base pretrain is counted separately. Token count and base both first-party.

Our estimate: large MoE, >20T tokens (official Qwen2.5-Max blog), active params undisclosed. Assuming ~57B active (the figure carried in the internal Holy Grail tab): 6 x 57e9 x 20e12 = 6.84e24. Arithmetic cross-check: VentureBeat (secondary) reports a 45-day pretrain on 2,048 GPUs, which is ~2.5x lower than 6.84e24 implies at H800 989 TFLOP/s x 35% MFU, so the 2,048 figure may be one stage or a higher-MFU run. Active-param count is the dominant unknown.
Estimated: 6 x 14e9 dense params x 20e12 tokens = 1.68e24 FLOP. Both figures official: 14B dense, trained from scratch on 20T tokens per the Baichuan-M1 technical report (arXiv 2502.12671).

Our estimate. Qwen2.5-VL technical report (arXiv:2502.13923) builds on the Qwen2.5-72B base (18T text tokens) plus a from-scratch dynamic-resolution ViT and multimodal pretraining. Full run dominated by the text base: 6 x 72e9 x 18e12 = 7.8e24; VL pretraining adds ~few x 1e23. Rounded to ~8e24 for the whole model. If only the VL adaptation stage is wanted (text base counted under Qwen2.5-72B, which is in Epoch), use ~7e23.
Estimated (6ND, MoE active): 16B total / 2.8B activated, trained on 5.7T tokens with the Muon optimizer (HF card + Muon scaling paper); 6 x 2.8e9 x 5.7e12 = 9.6e22.

Our rough estimate; video diffusion does not follow the 6ND LLM rule. Wan2.1 is a 14B-param diffusion transformer (Alibaba Wan blog/HF). For diffusion models, a common order-of-magnitude is C ~ 6 x N x (tokens-equivalent of training samples). With no published step count, we anchor to peer open video diffusion (e.g. ~1e23-3e23 FLOP scale for ~10B-param video DiTs trained on hundreds of millions of clips) and take ~1.5e23. Highly uncertain; not an official figure.

Assuming the same dataset size as for Qwen2.5 training (18T tokens):

6ND = 6 * 32500000000 parameters * 18 * 10^12 tokens =  3.51 × 10^24

'Speculative' confidence
using C = 6*ND, 6* 56 billion parameters * 16 trillion tokens = ~5.4e24 FLOP
Estimated (6ND, MoE active): 290B total / 28.8B activated, pre-trained on 9T tokens ('Every FLOP Counts' report); 6 x 28.8e9 x 9e12 = 1.56e24.
Estimated (6ND, MoE active): 16.8B total / 2.75B activated; the report co-trains Lite and Plus on the same corpus, so assume ~9T tokens; 6 x 2.75e9 x 9e12 = 1.49e23.

6 × 37B × 14.8T = 3.29e24 pretraining FLOPs, 1e22 posttrainining FLOPs - see doc below

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1C4zBvHh4UNnzaorwQSdG61zQJsBV9X3i3A70NeelxHY/edit?usp=sharing

Our estimate. Alibaba states the model trained on 18T text tokens + 800B image/video + 300B audio + 100B video-with-audio = ~19.2T total tokens over a 7B dense model: 6 x 7e9 x 19.2e12 = 8.06e23. Token totals are official (Alibaba Cloud blog); the 7B is dense.

When compared to Llama 3.1 405B, Pangu Ultra achieves better scores on most of the challenging benchmarks, while utilizing only about 29% of the training FLOPs required by Llama 405B.

Compute = 6 FLOP/token/param *  135e9 params *13.2e12 tokens = 1.069200e+25 FLOP
This is consistent with 29% of Llama 405B's compute: 3.8e25*0.29=1.1e25.

Estimated (6ND, MoE active, base + VL): Moonlight 16B/2.8B-active base (5.7T tokens -> 9.6e22) plus a vision-language joint-training stage. Report describes multi-stage VL pre-training; assume ~1.4T additional multimodal tokens at 2.8B active (6 x 2.8e9 x 1.4e12 = 2.4e22). Total ~1.2e23.
Estimated (6ND, dense): GLM-4-0414 release reports ~15T-token pre-training corpus for the 32B base; 6 x 32e9 x 15e12 = 2.88e24.

Our estimate. MoE 200B total / 20B active (ByteDance Seed blog). Token count not officially stated; ByteDance Seed base models are ~10-20T-token class. Taking ~12T tokens for the pretrained base under the reasoning model: 6 x 20e9 x 12e12 = 1.44e24. NOTE this is the base-pretrain estimate; the RL reasoning post-training adds an unpublished increment. Token count assumed, not first-party.
6 FLOP / parameter / token * 22*10^9 active parameters * 36000000000000 tokens = 4.752e+24 FLOP
Estimated (6ND, dense): MiMo-7B-Base trained from scratch on 25T tokens (tech report); 6 x 7e9 x 25e12 = 1.05e24.
989000000000000 FLOP / GPU / sec [H800, bf16 assumed] * 1300000 GPU-hours [see training time notes] * 3600 sec / hour * 0.3 [assumed utilization] = 1.388556e+24 FLOP

Estimates by Ege Erdil in Gradient Updates:
https://epoch.ai/gradient-updates/what-went-into-training-deepseek-r1
"A dataset size of 14.8 trillion tokens is reasonable and in line with other models of this scale. Assuming that’s valid, the pretraining of this model would have required 6 * (37 billion) * (14.8 trillion) = 3e24 FLOP. If we assume DeepSeek’s training cluster consists of H800s with the PCIe form factor, then each should be capable of 1.5e15 FP8 per second, and the implied model FLOP utilization (MFU) of DeepSeek v3’s 55 day training run ends up being around 23%."

6 FLOP/token/param * 14.8T tokens * 37B active params = 3.29e24 FLOP (pretraining)
1.2e23 FLOP (post-training)
6.1e23 FLOP (fine-tuning)

Total compute: 3.29e24 + 1.2e23 + 6.1e23 = 4.02e24

Built on MiniMax-Text-01 (same 456B/45.9B base, no new large pretrain) plus an RL/long-context extension. Pretrain FLOP ~= Text-01's 2.9e24; the disclosed RL run (512 H800 x 3 weeks, $534,700) adds only ~5e22 (512 x 504h x 989e12 x 0.35 = ~9e22 raw, less for RL). Total ~3.0e24.

Our estimate. Fine-grained MoE 80B total / 13B active (GitHub Tencent-Hunyuan/Hunyuan-A13B, MarkTechPost). Token count not officially stated; Tencent's open Hunyuan series typically pretrains on ~7T+ tokens, and the model card implies ~20T-class pretraining. Taking ~20T tokens: 6 x 13e9 x 20e12 = 1.56e24. Token count is the dominant unknown (assumed, not first-party).

Estimated: 6 x 47e9 active params x 5.6e12 tokens = 1.58e24 FLOP. Token count (5.6T) and 47B active / 424B total from the official ERNIE 4.5 technical report. Report states 47% MFU but no headline FLOP.
Estimated: 6 x 47e9 active params x 5.6e12 tokens = 1.58e24 FLOP. Text-only MoE variant, 300B total / 47B active, same 5.6T corpus per the ERNIE 4.5 technical report.
Estimated: 6 x 3e9 active params x 5.6e12 tokens = 1.01e23 FLOP. 21B total / 3B active MoE per official release; assumes same 5.6T corpus as the family (uncertain for the small variant).

Official figures from the tech report (arXiv 2505.21411): 16.5B activated params per token, 13T pretraining tokens. MoE convention 6 x active-params x tokens = 6 x 1.65e10 x 1.3e13 = 1.29e24 FLOP. Excludes post-training; pretrain only.
6 FLOP / parameter / token * 32 * 10^9 activated parameters * 15.5 * 10^12 tokens = 2.976e+24 FLOP
6 FLOP / parameter / token * 35 * 10^9 active parameters * 7.5 * 10^12 tokens = 1.575e+24 FLOP`
6 FLOP / parameter / token * 22*10^9 active parameters * 36000000000000 tokens = 4.752e+24 FLOP
6 FLOP / parameter / token * 22*10^9 active parameters * 36000000000000 tokens = 4.752e+24 FLOP

Our rough estimate. Wan2.2 adds a two-expert MoE diffusion design: 27B total / 14B active per denoising step, trained on 65.6% more images and 83.2% more videos than Wan2.1 (Alibaba Wan2.2 blog/HF). Scaling Wan2.1's ~1.5e23 by ~1.8x for the larger dataset and roughly comparable active-param compute per step gives ~3e23. Diffusion, no published step count; highly uncertain.

Estimated: 6 x 38e9 active params x 20e12 tokens = 4.56e24 FLOP. 321B total / 38B active MoE, trained on 20T+ tokens (incl. 4T multimodal) per StepFun's release and technical report (arXiv 2507.19427).
(6 FLOP/token/parameter) * (23T tokens) * (32B active parameters) = 4.42e24 FLOP

Estimated (6ND, MoE active, base + extension): V3 base (37B active x 14.8T = 3.29e24) plus DeepSeek's disclosed ~840B-token long-context/continued-pretrain extension (6 x 37e9 x 0.84e12 = 1.9e23); total ~3.5e24, rounded to 3.7e24 to include the V3.1 hybrid-reasoning extension.
6 FLOP/parameter/token * 27000000000 active parameters * 23000000000000 tokens ["Likely" confidence, see dataset size notes] = 3.726e+24 FLOP

6ND with:
36T tokens is taken from the qwen3 technical report
70B active params is based on it having >1T params, and the architectures of Qwen3-235B-A22B and Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B
6.48e+23 FLOP [base model compute] + 5.67e+21 FLOP = 6.5367e+23 FLOP
6 FLOP/parameter/token * 3000000000 active parameters * 2000000000000 tokens = 3.6e+22 FLOP

Our estimate. Qwen3-VL technical report (arXiv:2511.21631): flagship is 235B total / 22B active MoE built on the Qwen3 backbone. Assuming the VL training mirrors Qwen3-235B-A22B's ~36T tokens at 22B active: 6 x 22e9 x 36e12 = 4.75e24. Token count assumed equal to the Qwen3 text base (report does not separately state VL token volume); active-param count is official.

6 FLOP/parameter/token * 50000000000 active parameters * 20000000000000 tokens = 6e+24 FLOP

Assuming the additional post-training contributed between 1% and 100% of Kimi K2's training compute (which we confidently estimate at 2.976e+24), we get a range of 3.0e24 to 6.0e24, and a geometric mean of 4.2e24.

Training tokens not disclosed.

Presuming this is a post-training update from GLM-4.5 (23T tokens) with minor additional compute, training compute is at least:

6 FLOP/parameter/token * 32B active parameters * 23T tokens = 4.42e24 FLOP
Estimated: 6 x 11e9 active params x 17.2e12 tokens = 1.14e24 FLOP. 196B total / 11B active MoE, 17.2T tokens per StepFun (arXiv 2602.10604). Date is month-only (YYYY-MM-01).

Per section 3.2, "The joint pre-training stage continues from a near-end Kimi K2 checkpoint over additional 15T vision-text tokens at 4K sequence length"

Kimi K2 was pretrained on 15T tokens. A near-end checkpoint of K2 was trained into K2.5 over another 15T tokens (or 15.2 to 15.5T tokens). This makes for a total of ~30T.

There are an additional 1T tokens in "Stage 1" used to train the ViT encoder, which is likely much smaller than the full LLM, so we omit those tokens to calculate training compute.

30e12 tokens * 32B parameters * 6 = 5.76e24 FLOP
